title: Agent Spec Protocol v1 — Full Schema
description: Validates the complete ASP document structure (all 12 sections). For lightweight agents needing only sections 1-4, use asp-minimal.schema.json.

model_tier.description: Model tier assignment per Part 4 Model Selection

schedule.description: When this agent is active. Triggered and periodic agents should describe their trigger/cadence in the Identity section.

extends.description: Path to a base ASP document to inherit from. Sections in this document override the base. Null means no inheritance.

identity.description: Section 2: Who this agent is. Second-person prose ('You are the...'). 1-3 sentences defining the agent's self-concept.

scope.description: Section 3: Exhaustive list of responsibilities. If it's not listed, the agent doesn't do it.

must_not.description: What this agent must never attempt

handled_by.description: Which agent or role handles this responsibility instead

boundaries.description: Section 4: Hard constraints on what the agent does NOT do. Each boundary must specify who handles the excluded responsibility.

when.description: Condition under which output is routed here

output_routing.description: Section 5: Where outputs go and under what conditions. No orphaned outputs.

context_profile.description: Section 6: Context requirements at three tiers

standing.description: Always loaded — documents/artifacts needed in every session

session.description: Provided per task by the Orchestrator

input.description: What the receiving side gets — format, required fields

interface_contracts.description: Section 7: Input/output schemas for each connection declared in output_routing

decision.description: The specific decision this agent may face

tier.description: Authority tier from Part 3: enforced (hooks decide), autonomous (agent decides), propose (agent recommends), escalate (stop and flag)

brain_query.description: Whether the agent should consult the Brain before this decision

decision_authority.description: Section 8: Per-decision tier assignments. Authority governs who decides, not what blocks — see Part 3 for enforcement.

context_discovery.description: Section 9: How the agent learns project-specific information at deployment time (SO-11)

must_learn.description: Project-specific data the agent must acquire before operating

discovery_questions.description: Questions to resolve before producing output

if_missing.description: What to do if required context is unavailable

blast_radius.description: What damage this agent could cause if wrong

bias_risks.description: Specific biases this agent is susceptible to

human_review_triggers.description: When this agent must recommend human review

rag_grounding.description: What the agent must verify when retrieving from the Brain

prompt_anchor.description: Section 11: 1-3 sentence north star. Loaded last in the system prompt. Direct address to the agent.

liveness.description: Section 12: Heartbeat and failover specification (command agents only)

failure_threshold.description: Consecutive missed heartbeats before failover triggers

monitored_by.description: Agents or roles that monitor this heartbeat
